title: KnowledgeEntry
description: Output of Agent: knowledge-manager. A durable institutional-memory record (decision logged, incident, milestone retrospective, or weekly review takeaway) captured from project events for future PMs to search 6, 12, 18 months from now. Extends pm_artifact_base — same top-level shape, with metadata.* tightened to the knowledge entry index fields. Lane 1 (auto-archive) by default; the runtime forces tier-0 review on confidence < 0.70 or when escalation_reasons names a controversial-decision flag.

trigger.description: Why this knowledge entry exists. 'decision_logged' = a deliberate choice was made and we want to remember it + its rationale. 'incident' = something went wrong (or nearly did) and we want to capture the lesson. 'milestone' = a major event landed (good or bad) and we want the post-event reflection. 'weekly_review' = recurring rollup of what we learned this week.

decision_or_lesson.description: The single durable takeaway, as plain English a future PM could act on without context. For decision_logged: the decision + the reasoning. For incident: the lesson learned. For milestone: the pattern to repeat or avoid. For weekly_review: the summary of the week's most reusable insight.

context_summary.description: One- to two-paragraph summary of the situation that produced the lesson. Names projects, dates, scope. Plain narrative voice. PII is redacted before this field is written.

applicable_phases.description: Construction phases this entry is relevant to. A future PM searching for 'what did we learn during MEP rough-in' should find this if 'mep_rough_in' is here. Multiple phases allowed when a lesson crosses phase boundaries.

applicable_disciplines.description: Disciplines this entry touches (e.g. 'structural', 'electrical', 'mechanical', 'safety', 'procurement', 'controls', 'commissioning', 'civil', 'life_safety', 'controls_integration'). Free-form lowercase tags; the knowledge index treats these as facets.

search_tags.description: Lowercase, hyphen-separated search tags. The knowledge index uses these for full-text and faceted search. Examples: 'long-lead', 'chiller-substitution', 'lockout-tagout', 'commissioning-stall', 'owner-directive'. Always include enough tags that a future PM searching for 'what did we do about chiller delays' finds this entry.

related_artifact_ids.description: Artifact IDs (RFIs, submittals, PCOs, prior knowledge entries, status updates, DFRs, etc.) that back the claims in this entry. Every substantive claim in body_markdown must trace to one of these (or to a citation in the top-level citations[] field).

decision_owner.description: Role of the person who owned the decision or lesson — e.g. 'owner_pm', 'gc_super', 'mep_lead', 'commissioning_agent', 'safety_director'. ALWAYS a role, never a personal name. If only a name is in the input, redact to role + flag pii_redacted in escalation_reasons.

decision_date.description: Inclusive ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the decision was made or the event occurred. Drives chronological ordering in the knowledge index.
